required_skills: Aerospace structural analysis including static, dynamic, and buckling analysis
Finite element analysis (FEA) modeling and simulation for metallic and composite structures
Fatigue and fracture mechanics analysis including damage tolerance and crack growth prediction
Composite material design and analysis (laminate theory, ply stacking, failure criteria)
Structural sizing and margin of safety (MOS) calculations per aerospace standard practices
Loads and aeroelastic analysis including limit load, ultimate load, and gust load derivation
Metallic and non-metallic material characterization and selection for aerospace applications
Drawing interpretation and geometric dimensioning and tolerancing (GD&T)
Structural test planning, instrumentation, and correlation of FEA models to test data
Design for manufacturability and producibility of aerospace structural assemblies
preferred_skills: Experience with additive manufacturing (AM) and topology optimization for structural components
Knowledge of NASA-STD-5020 or ECSS structural standards for space structure qualification
Familiarity with MIL-HDBK-17 composite material design allowables and qualification methods
Experience with acoustic and random vibration fatigue analysis
Thermal-structural coupled analysis for re-entry or high-temperature structural applications
Knowledge of bonded joint analysis and repair methodology for composite airframe structures
Stress corrosion cracking and environmental degradation assessment for long-life aerospace structures
responsibilities: Develop and execute FEA models in NASTRAN or Abaqus to perform static, dynamic, buckling, and modal analyses of primary and secondary aerospace structural components
Calculate structural margins of safety for metallic and composite structures under limit and ultimate design loads per FAA FAR 25, MIL-SPEC, or NASA standards
Perform fatigue life estimation and fracture mechanics crack growth analyses using NASGRO or AFGROW to support damage tolerance assessments and inspection intervals
Design composite laminates using classical lamination theory, selecting ply orientations and material systems to meet stiffness, strength, and weight requirements
Derive and apply structural loads from aerodynamic, inertial, acoustic, and thermal environments to size structural members and validate load paths
Correlate and update FEA models against ground vibration test (GVT) and static test data to improve model fidelity and reduce analytical uncertainty
Collaborate with manufacturing and producibility teams to ensure structural designs are optimized for fabrication using conventional and additive manufacturing processes
Author detailed stress reports, design allowables data packages, and structural substantiation reports to support airworthiness certification and design reviews
Conduct trade studies on material selection (aluminum alloys, titanium, CFRP) to optimize structural mass, cost, and mechanical performance for mission requirements
Support structural testing campaigns including coupon, element, and full-scale article testing by defining test setups, fixture designs, and acceptance criteria
